title: auto_learn: detect_preferences records on every user message whose lowered text starts with always/never/prefer/use/don't — false-positive prone
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness. Location: src/auto_learn.rs:275-314 (detect_preferences). Violation: The function flags any message under 200 chars starting with 'always ', 'never ', 'prefer ', 'use ', 'dont use ' as a preference; any 300-char message starting with 'no,', 'wrong', 'dont', 'stop', 'actually,', 'instead,' as a correction. Examples that silently pollute the preference DB: 'use the read_file tool to check config.yaml', 'prefer we skip testing for now', 'never mind, try a different approach'. Contextual intent is lost. This adds garbage to learned_preferences that later gets injected into every system prompt as 'Learned Preferences'. Mandated Refactor: Gate with an explicit heuristic: require the user message to be exclusively a preference statement (no tool invocations, no imperatives to the agent). Better: make this model-in-the-loop — periodically ask a small model to summarize session-long 'preferences the user stated' rather than regex-triggering. Or require an explicit /prefer slash command for recording.
